data.title: Stocks little changed at the open in truncated Christmas Eve trading session
data.description:
U.S. stocks opened largely flat on Wednesday, kicking off a shortened regular trading session due to Christmas Eve.

Markets will close at 1300 ET today and will be shut on Thursday.

The Dow (DJI) rose +0.05% to 48,467.49 points, while the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was down -0.01% at 6,909.23 points. The Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) fell -0.09% to 23,541.38 points.

Traders received some labor market data in the form of an unexpectedly lower initial jobless claims report. 

A blowout U.S. Q3 GDP report lifted sentiment in the previous session and helped Wall Street notch its 38th record close of the year, underscoring the stock market's resilience after a turbulent 2025.

See the chart below shared by Charlie Bilello, chief market strategist at Creative Planning, on X:

Wednesday also marks the start of a period that has traditionally resulted in gains for equities, leading to a Santa Claus rally - a historical trend that has seen the benchmark S&P 500 index (SP500) post gains during the last five trading days of the year and the first two of the next year.

Treasury yields showed mixed movement in early trading. The shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) ticked up to 3.54%, while the benchmark 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) slipped to 4.16%. The longer-dated 30-year Treasury yield (US30Y) also edged lower to 4.82%.

Top gainers included Nike (NKE) +3.99%, Sandisk (SNDK) +3.32%, and Danaher (DHR) +1.24%. Decliners included Paycom Software (PAYC) -3.50%, Intel (INTC) -2.86%, and Datadog (DDOG) -2.85%.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Wednesday: DVAX, NKE, INTC, and more
data.description:
Stock futures edged modestly higher on Wednesday, building on the S&P 500's fresh record close from Tuesday, ahead of an early market shutdown at 1:00 p.m. ET for Christmas Eve.

Here are some of Wednesday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Dynavax Technologies (DVAX) +39% - Shares surged after Sanofi (SNY) agreed to acquire the vaccine maker for about $2.2B, or $15.50 per share in cash, representing a 39% premium to its December 23 close. This deal strengthens Sanofi’s adult immunization portfolio with Dynavax’s marketed HEPLISAV-B hepatitis B vaccine and its phase 1/2 shingles candidate Z-1018. Sanofi will fund the transaction entirely with cash and launch a tender offer followed by a merger if successful. It expects no impact on its 2025 guidance and anticipates closing in Q1 2026, with Dynavax CEO Ryan Spencer saying the combination will accelerate the global reach and impact of the company’s vaccine portfolio.
  • Agios Pharmaceuticals (AGIO) +15% - Shares soared after the U.S. FDA approved AQVESME (mitapivat) for the treatment of anemia in adults with alpha- or beta-thalassemia, making it the first and only FDA-approved therapy for anemia in both transfusion-dependent and non-transfusion-dependent patients with these conditions. The approval was based on positive results from the Phase 3 ENERGIZE and ENERGIZE-T trials, which showed clinically meaningful benefits across both patient populations. Due to REMS requirements, mitapivat will be marketed as AQVESME in the U.S. for thalassemia while continuing as PYRUKYND for PK deficiency, with a U.S. launch expected in late January 2026 following REMS implementation.
  • UiPath (PATH) +7% - Shares climbed after S&P Dow Jones Indices announced the automation software company will join the S&P MidCap 400, replacing Synovus Financial ((SNV)), effective January 2, 2026, following Synovus’s pending acquisition by Pinnacle Financial Partners ((PNFP)), a move that is expected to drive incremental demand from index-tracking funds.
  • Ramaco Resources (METC) +6% - Shares advanced after the company announced a new $100M share repurchase program authorized by its board, allowing buybacks of Class A common stock over the next 24 months, with flexibility to use Rule 10b5-1 trading plans. Management said the move reflects confidence in Ramaco’s operational performance and financial strength, following more than $600M in capital raised in the second half of 2025, and emphasized that repurchases will be made opportunistically while preserving liquidity to fund ongoing capital development projects.
  • Nike (NKE) +3% - Shares rose and drew investor attention after a regulatory filing showed notable insider buying, led by Apple CEO and Nike’s lead independent director Tim Cook, who purchased nearly $3M worth of stock. According to an SEC Form 4, Cook bought 50,000 Class B shares on December 22 at an average price of $58.97, taking his direct holdings to 105,480 shares and signaling confidence in Nike’s long-term outlook. Adding to the bullish insider signal, another Nike director and former Intel CEO Robert Swan also bought 8,691 shares the same day at an average price of $57.54, reinforcing optimism around the embattled sportswear company.

Biggest stock losers

  • Intel (INTC) -3% - Shares plunged amid reports Nvidia has halted testing of Intel’s advanced 18A chip manufacturing process, signaling it may not move forward with producing chips on the technology despite earlier evaluations. The decision comes just months after the companies announced a major partnership under which Intel would develop custom x86 CPUs for Nvidia’s AI infrastructure and system-on-chips integrating Nvidia RTX GPU chiplets. As part of that deal, Nvidia had also agreed to invest $5B in Intel stock, subject to regulatory approvals, making the pause in 18A testing a setback to expectations around Intel’s foundry ambitions.

data.title: OpenAI making progress with advertising push: report
data.description:
OpenAI (OPENAI) appears to be making progress with its push to show advertising inside its near-ubiquitous ChatGPT chatbot, The Information reported. 

Internal discussions amongst OpenAI employees have included how to tweak certain artificial intelligence models to prioritize sponsored information in ChatGPT's responses, the news outlet added, citing a person familiar with the discussions. Additional discussions have also included recent mockups on the variety of ways ads could appear inside the app, a person who had seen the mockups told the news outlet.

The company may also create a new type of digital ad, another person told the news outlet. Right now, the digital advertising market is dominated by Google (GOOG) (GOOGL), Meta Platforms (META) and Amazon (AMZN), which collectively account for more than 50% of the market.

“As ChatGPT becomes more capable and widely used, we're looking at ways to continue offering more intelligence to everyone,” an OpenAI spokesperson told Seeking Alpha. “As part of this, we're exploring what ads in our product could look like. People have a trusted relationship with ChatGPT, and any approach would be designed to respect that trust.”

OpenAI’s CEO of Applications Fidji Simo has been looking for someone to oversee monetization efforts, including bringing advertising to ChatGPT. It was reported in September that Simo had met with potential candidates, including some of her former colleagues from Facebook to lead a new team that will bring ads to ChatGPT.

OpenAI CFO Sarah Friar told the Financial Times last December that the ChatGPT maker was exploring the addition of advertising to its products.

A push into advertising could further boost OpenAI's revenue, especially important as the company looks set to raise another significant funding round, perhaps gathering another $100B from investors. In November, OpenAI CEO Sam Altman said the ChatGPT maker was doing “well more” than $13B in revenue and suggested it could hit $100B in revenue in 2027. OpenAI currently generates revenue from selling subscriptions to ChatGPT to consumers and businesses alike, as well as providing access to its application programming interface and other tools.

data.title: Medicare pilot programs aim to reduce prescription drug spend
data.description:
A pair of pilot programs recently introduced by CMS aims to reduce Medicare spending on prescription drugs as well as lowering out-of-pocket costs for beneficiaries.

The programs, known as the Guarding U.S. Medicare Against Rising Drug Costs (GUARD) and Global Benchmark for Efficient Drug Pricing (GLOBE) models, were announced on Dec. 19, the same day President Trump announced drug pricing deals under his "most favored nation" policy with nine additional drugmakers. 

The GUARD program would mandate that pharmas pay rebates for drugs covered under Part D if the prices are more than those paid in other economically comparable countries. It would run from Jan. 1, 2027-Dec. 31, 2031.

GLOBE is similar to GUARD, but applies to drugs under Part B. 

The rebates are "an alternative to the manufacturer rebates currently calculated under the Medicare Inflation Rebate Programs, which were established by the Inflation Reduction Act of 2022 (IRA) and require manufacturers to pay rebates to the Medicare Trust Fund for certain drugs whose prices grow faster than inflation," according to law firm Mintz.

The Pharmaceutical Research and Manufacturers of America (PhRMA), the lobbying group for major drugmakers, has objected to the programs.

"Government policies that mandate broad-based foreign price controls are bad for American patients and undermine U.S. leadership," the organization said in a statement. "By CMS' own admission, the proposed policies are projected to increase costs for America’s seniors, and they will siphon billions from U.S. medicine R&D at a time when China is on the verge of surpassing us."

data.title: Building products stocks stack gains for the industrial sector in 2025
data.description:
The industrials sector has emerged as one of the top-performing segments of the broader S&P 500 (SP500) in 2025, supported by strength across several key industries. Among the most notable contributors to the sector’s success has been the building products industry.

Continued infrastructure investment, resilient construction demand, and easing supply-chain pressures have helped drive revenue growth and margin expansion for many companies in the space. These favorable dynamics have translated into strong stock performance and renewed investor interest. 

Outlined below are the top 10 performing building products stocks for the 2025 trading year. Moreover, the curated list focuses exclusively on companies with market capitalizations of at least $1 billion. 

No. 1: Resideo Technologies (REZI), +54.6% YTD.

No. 2: Johnson Controls International (JCI), +53.5% YTD. 

No. 3: AZZ (AZZ), +35.2% YTD.

No. 4: Armstrong World Industries (AWI), +34.8% YTD. 

No. 5: Advanced Drainage Systems (WMS), +28.8% YTD. 

No. 6: Zurn Elkay Water Solutions (ZWS), +28.1% YTD.

No. 7: Allegion (ALLE), +22.9% YTD. 

No. 8: Modine Manufacturing (MOD), +18.7% YTD. 

No. 9: Trane Technologies (TT), +6.6% YTD.

No. 10: Griffon Corporation (GFF), +5.5% YTD. 

Industrials ETFs: (XLI), (VIS), (FXR), (IYJ), (FIDU), and (RSPN).

data.title: Omeros wins FDA nod for transplant therapy
data.description:
Omeros Corporation (OMER) announced on Wednesday that the U.S. FDA approved its lead asset, narsoplimab-wuug, as a treatment for hematopoietic stem cell transplant-associated thrombotic microangiopathy, a complication of stem cell transplantation.

The treatment branded as Yartemlea is expected to be available next month, with its U.S. billing and reimbursement codes currently in place, the Seattle, Washington-based biotech said while its shares remained on hold for trading in anticipation of the decision.  

“After years of work and close collaboration with the transplant community, we can now offer the first FDA-approved therapy for this frequently fatal complication, with robust response data and a benefit-risk profile that supports confident use in both adults and children,” added CEO Gregory Demopulos.

In May, the FDA accepted for review Omeros’ (OMER) resubmitted Biologics License Application for narsoplimab-wuug for TA-TMA, setting Sept. 25 as the target action date. Subsequently, the regulator delayed the review, extending the target action date to Dec. 26 following additional data provided by the company in response to an FDA information request.

A marketing authorization application for Yartemlea is currently undergoing review in Europe, with a decision expected in mid-2026.

data.title: Northcoast sees EPS growth and expansion earning Costco a Buy rating
data.description:
Costco’s (COST) earnings are projected to grow by roughly 20% over the next two years, driven by its competitive position among retailers and the momentum of its wholesale club channel. As a result, the stock earned an upgrade to Buy from Neutral at Northcoast Research with a $1,100 price target, implying 29% upside to Tuesday’s close.  

“[The 21% pullback in shares since February] begs the question, should the shares of this outstanding retailer be purchased? Our answer is yes,” said Northcoast’s Chuck Cerankosky. “The P/E may get a little bit cheaper, but we are confident in steady EPS growth and the shares advancing to our $1,100 price objective.”

Besides the company’s competitive advantage in the U.S., Costco (COST) has considerable opportunities for chain expansion globally. It’s international development from South Korea to Sweden has been “consistently successful,”

“Developed economies contain consumers who love to save money and in turn spend it on discretionary merchandise. Members can do both inside a Costco club,” Cerankosky adds.

More importantly for current shareholders, Cerankosky notes the company’s cash reserves which provides the resources to pay another special dividend of perhaps as much as $20 per share.

Since the beginning of the year, Costco (COST) shares are down 7% compared to a 17.5% gain in the S&P 500 but has outperformed the S&P 500 over a 5-year period.

data.title: Hologic says 2026 CVR is more challenging with stopped shipment of product
data.description:
Hologic (HOLX) said that achieving the 2026 milestone under its  CVR agreement with its sale to Blackstone (BX)  and TPG (TPG) will be more challenging after it stopped shipment of a product.

In November, following an internal review of post-market data, Hologic initiated a stop ship of its Brevera Breast Biopsy System Disposable 9 Gauge Needles, Hologic said in a  proxy filing on Tuesday. The company expects that the stop ship could be prolonged.

"In light of the Company’s inability to presently predict the duration of the stop ship, the Company believes that it will be more challenging to achieve the 2026 milestone under the CVR agreement," Hologic said in the filing.

As part of Hologic's $18.3 billion sale to Blackstone and TPG, there is a CVR, or contingent value right,  that allows investors to receive up to $3 per share in two equal payments if the company’s Breast Health business achieves certain global sales milestones in fiscal years 2026 and 2027.

data.title: From rockets to radar, the aerospace and defense industry soars in 2025
data.description:
The aerospace and defense industry, a cornerstone of the industrials sector, delivered an exceptional showing in 2025, handily outperforming both its parent sector and the broader equity market.

Supported by sustained defense spending, rising commercial aerospace demand, and expanding space-related investment, the group benefited from powerful tailwinds throughout the year.

As the calendar turns toward year-end, aerospace and defense stands out as one of the market’s strongest performers, with the industry being +48.5% year to date. That gain far exceeds the industrials sector’s +18.5% advance and comfortably surpasses the S&P 500’s (SP500) +17% rise, underscoring the group’s relative strength.

Within this favorable environment, several companies delivered outsized returns. The list below highlights the 10 best-performing U.S.-listed aerospace and defense stocks of 2025, focusing exclusively on companies with market capitalizations of at least $1 billion. 

No. 1: Astronics (ATRO), +246.8% YTD.

No. 2: Kratos Defense & Security Solutions (KTOS), +212.0% YTD.

No. 3: Rocket Lab (RKLB), +203.0% YTD.

No. 4: ATI (ATI), +111.1% YTD. 

No. 5: Carpenter Technology (CRS), +96.5% YTD.

No. 6: Howmet Aerospace (HWM), +91.6% YTD. 

No. 7: Woodward (WWD), +90.0% YTD.

No. 8: General Electric (GE), +89.2% YTD. 

No. 9: VSE Corporation (VSEC), +88.1% YTD. 

No. 10: Huntington Ingalls Industries (HII), +87.6% YTD. 

Aerospace & Defense: (ITA), (PPA), (XAR), (SHLD), (FITE), and (DFEN).

Industrials ETFs: (XLI), (VIS), (FXR), (IYJ), (FIDU), and (RSPN).

data.title: Eastern International announces photovoltaic power generation construction project contract
data.description:
• Eastern International (ELOG) on Wednesday said its wholly-owned subsidiary, Guizhou Tianrun Zhicheng Construction Engineering has entered into a construction contract with Weifang Branch of CSCEC Southwest Architecture & Design Institute Design Consulting for its Hebei Laiyuan 300 MW Centralized Photovoltaic Power Generation Project.
  • Pursuant to the contract, Guizhou Tianrun will act as a subcontractor and be responsible for the construction of Phase I - 50 MW of the Centralized Photovoltaic Power Generation Project, with a total contract price of RMB 42.5 million.
  • Guizhou Tianrun will be responsible for the foundation works, photovoltaic mounting structure installation, photovoltaic module installation, module string wiring, installation and wiring of photovoltaic combiner boxes and supply of certain auxiliary materials.
  • The project is scheduled to commence for construction before December 31, 2025.
  • ELOG +42.95% premarket to $2.03.
  • Source: Press Release
